title: Loan Underwriting: The debt service coverage ratio (DSCR) is calculated by dividing...
summary: Calculate NOI by subtracting operating expenses from rental income.
prompt: The debt service coverage ratio (DSCR) is calculated by dividing the net operating income (NOI) by the total debt service.
solution: True. The Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR) is a financial metric used to assess a property’s ability to generate enough income to cover its debt obligations. It is calculated by dividing the Net Operating Income (NOI) by the total debt service. A DSCR greater than 1 indicates that the property generates sufficient income to cover its debt payments, while a DSCR less than 1 indicates a potential shortfall. This metric is critical for investors and lenders in evaluating the financial viability of a property.
